title: Cyperus cayennensis
taxonomy: Cyperus cayennensis is classified as a species of sedge, belonging to the family Cyperaceae, which is a large and diverse group of monocotyledonous plants. This family comprises around 5,000 to 6,000 species, with Cyperus being one of the most common and widespread genera. As a member of the Cyperaceae family, Cyperus cayennensis is further grouped into the subfamily Cyperoideae and the tribe Cyperae. Within the tribe Cyperae, Cyperus cayennensis is part of the subtribe Cyperinae. The classification of Cyperus cayennensis reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other sedge species.
uses: Cyperus cayennensis, a species of sedge, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's rhizomes and roots have been employed to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. In some cultures, extracts from Cyperus cayennensis are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate joint pain and swelling. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cyperus cayennensis for medicinal purposes. However, its traditional uses demonstrate the plant's potential as a valuable resource in folk medicine.

side_effects: Cyperus cayennensis is commonly known as the Cayenne sedge or Cape sedge. Unfortunately, information on the specific side effects of Cyperus cayennensis is limited. intro: Cyperus cayennensis, also known as the Cayenne sedge, is a type of sedge plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties and ability to reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Cyperus cayennensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cyperus cayennensis is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of a syrup or tincture to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
